summary: Full E2E validation of new Digital Products (PDF) feature. 15/15 pytest pass + admin & customer UI verified end-to-end on the public preview URL. Backend: all 11 endpoints work (admin upload/list/delete/entitlements, customer info/download), enforce 24h window (410 on expired), 3-download cap (403 on 4th), reject non-PDF (400), and properly authenticate via Bearer admin token (401 without token). create_entitlements_for_order helper correctly resolves product_sku→product_id, filters digital-only items, and inserts a row in digital_downloads with secure token. Regression: GET /api/products still returns ≥15 items (5 with productType='digital') and /api/mollie-status returns 'ok'. Frontend: /admin/digital-products renders Upload form, file list (5 files) and Token table; /mijn-download/{token} shows product name, '3 / 3 over' counter, expiry date '18-05-2026', and clicking 'Download PDF' triggers signed-URL fetch + decrements counter to '2 / 3 over' with 'Download gestart!' confirmation.

minor.issue: Counter increment is NOT atomic (read-then-write). Under concurrent requests on the same token the same value (used+1) could be written twice, effectively allowing a 4th download in a tight race. Use Supabase RPC or SQL UPDATE ... SET downloads_used = downloads_used + 1 WHERE downloads_used < max_downloads RETURNING * to make it atomic.

Recursive listing assumes max 2-level folder depth (root/library or root/products/<id>/file). Files placed in deeper subfolders would be invisible. Acceptable for current layout but document or enforce.

If insert into digital_downloads fails for one item, others still attempt — but the partial state is silently logged. Webhook returns 200 regardless. For idempotency consider also de-duplicating on (order_id, product_id) so a Mollie webhook retry does not create duplicate tokens / duplicate emails.
Unused imports: smtplib, ssl, MIMEText, MIMEMultipart (all email now via Resend through _send_email shim). Cosmetic.

integration_issues.flow: Header product dropdown elsewhere in app
integration_issues.issue: Multiple console errors 'Error fetching products, using fallback: TypeError: Failed to fetch' appeared during /admin/digital-products load. The admin page itself works (uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL correctly); the noise originates from a different component that still fetches from an unreachable URL. Not blocking, but should be traced and fixed.

issues: Looks clean and on-brand; counter, expiry date and download CTA are clearly visible. No issues.

action_items: (Recommend) Make downloads_used increment atomic to close the race-condition window described above.
(Recommend) De-duplicate entitlements in create_entitlements_for_order on (order_id, product_id) so Mollie webhook retries don't duplicate tokens/emails.
(Optional) Trace and silence 'Error fetching products, using fallback' console errors that fire on the admin page from an unrelated component.
(Optional) Remove unused smtplib/ssl/MIMEText/MIMEMultipart imports from routes/orders_supabase.py.
critical_code_review_comments: digital_products.py: clean ~400-line module with clear separation of admin vs customer endpoints; good use of HTTPBearer dependency. Async signatures are correct, no _id leakage.
Token generation uses secrets.token_urlsafe(32) → 256 bits of entropy, OK.
Signed-URL lifetime is 5 minutes which is appropriate for direct-download UX; not exposed to URL guessing.
Bucket name 'digital-products' assumed PRIVATE — verified via signed-URL flow returning a `token=` parameter; direct public access would not be possible.
Webhook handler in orders_supabase.py correctly calls create_entitlements_for_order only on new_status=='paid', and emails the customer with one download link per entitlement. Good UX.
Race condition on counter increment is the only meaningful concern; everything else is cosmetic.
test_credentials: admin / Droomvriendjes2024! via POST /api/admin/login (returns {token}). Admin UI reads from localStorage key 'admin_token'.
seed_data_creation: Test inserted ~6 TEST_-prefixed rows into digital_downloads during the run (3 token-counter test, 1 expired test, 1 helper-flow test, 1 frontend test). All cleaned up after the run via DELETE WHERE download_token LIKE 'TEST_%' — 0 leftovers. The smoke-test PDF uploaded to Supabase Storage was also deleted via the admin delete endpoint.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Digital Products endpoints under /api/digital-products/* are green. Pytest suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_digital_products.py and uses BASE_URL from REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL + reads SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY from /app/backend/.env directly. To re-test, run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_digital_products.py -v. Important: tests insert rows into digital_downloads with TEST_ prefixed download_token and TEST_ prefixed order_id; the test_zzz_cleanup_test_entitlements step removes them in-session. If the test is aborted mid-flight, run the same cleanup snippet from test_reports/iteration_26 (DELETE FROM digital_downloads WHERE download_token LIKE 'TEST_%'). Frontend admin route /admin/digital-products requires localStorage.setItem('admin_token', <token from /api/admin/login>) — there is no automatic redirect to login on the page itself.
rca of the issue: N/A — this is feature validation, not a bug-fix iteration. No product code was modified.
